name: HSV1_IRL_IRS_inverted_repeats
description: HSV-1 inverted repeat regions: the alphaherpesvirus genome is organized as two unique segments UL and US, each flanked by inverted repeats (TRL, IRL flanking UL; IRS, TRS flanking US). The IRL/IRS repeats permit UL/US inversion to produce four genomic isomers in equimolar proportion. Repeats contain the latency-associated transcript (LAT) locus, ICP0 and ICP4 immediate-early genes, and the 'a' sequence (200-500 bp) that mediates inversion and packaging. Note: HSV-1 is not in the primary user-requested type list but is included as the canonical exemplar of herpesvirus IRL/IRS architecture referenced in the user scope.
expected_copy_count: 2 copies each of IRL/TRL (UL flanks) and IRS/TRS (US flanks)
